title: Prove a Claim
subtitle: A small starting shape for work where a single assertion and the strength of its proof need to stay explicit.
scope: One truth claim, model claim, or parser claim that needs to be defended with staged evidence.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholder primitives, evidence, and tickets with real proof sources.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when you need to make one important claim explicit and show how strong the current proof actually is.
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Start with one explicit claim and a staged proof path; do not let the claim, current evidence, and conclusions blur together.

flowchart LR status_snapshot["status-snapshot

Track the evidence rungs that support or weaken the assertion."] status_snapshot -- "summarizes" --> model_assertion model_assertion -- "requires-proof" --> proof_ladder

semanticDefinition.objectiveRole: Seed a proof doc by separating the claim from its proof ladder.
semanticDefinition.templateSubtitle: A small starting shape for work where a single assertion and the strength of its proof need to stay explicit.
semanticDefinition.templateScope: One truth claim, model claim, or parser claim that needs to be defended with staged evidence.
semanticDefinition.templateObjective: Make the main claim and its current proof strength visible without collapsing claim, evidence, and conclusions together.
semanticDefinition.templateSuccessCondition: A reader can say what is being claimed, what evidence currently supports it, and what stronger proof would look like next.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.role: Summarize proof posture without carrying the claim or evidence itself.
sections.rationale: This section keeps claim readiness visible while the assertion and proof ladder remain distinct.

sections.title: Model Assertion
sections.role: State the claim being defended or weakened.
sections.rationale: The claim needs its own card so proof strength can change without rewriting what is being claimed.

sections.title: Proof Ladder
sections.role: Track the evidence rungs that support or weaken the assertion.
sections.rationale: Proof strength needs staged rungs so current evidence and next proof are visible separately from the assertion.

relationships.rationale: Proof status should summarize the actual claim under review.
A claim cannot be treated as operational truth without staged proof strength.

evidence.description: A proof rung should identify the claim, primitive, invariant, or authority it supports or challenges.

stageSignals.question: What exact claim should this proof doc defend or weaken?

stageSignals.when: model-assertion exists but proof-ladder has no corresponding proof rungs
stageSignals.question: Which proof rung directly supports or challenges the claim?

stageSignals.when: assertion and proof ladder are populated and linked
stageSignals.question: What stronger proof would move this claim beyond its current status?

validOperations.label: Add assertion
validOperations.description: Add the claim being defended, with primitives, invariants, or authorities if known.

validOperations.label: Add proof rung
validOperations.description: Add evidence that supports, weakens, or scopes the assertion.

objective: Make the main claim and its current proof strength visible without collapsing claim, evidence, and conclusions together.
successCondition: A reader can say what is being claimed, what evidence currently supports it, and what stronger proof would look like next.

callouts.title: How to use this starter
items: Keep the assertion singular. If you are proving several independent things, split them into multiple docs or multiple later sections.
Use the proof ladder to show increasing strength, not just a pile of tests.

sections.title: Status Snapshot

callout.title: Starter intent
items: Use the snapshot to make proof strength legible at a glance.
Let the sections below carry the actual evidence structure.

sections.rationale: This section keeps claim readiness visible while the assertion and proof ladder remain distinct.

sections.title: Model Assertion

data.name: Canonical truth path
notes: Write the one claim the rest of the document is trying to defend.

sections.rationale: The claim needs its own card so proof strength can change without rewriting what is being claimed.

sections.title: Proof Ladder

data.name: Frozen evidence replay
notes: Start with the smallest rerunnable rung that other people can inspect without interpretation drift.

data.name: Production drift detection
notes: Reserve higher rungs for evidence that gets closer to live operational truth.

sections.rationale: Proof strength needs staged rungs so current evidence and next proof are visible separately from the assertion.
